summary: PR #2504 improved matching in the lbmthp library translation unit by typing lbmthp static data and movie-player/THP decode fields, refactoring several helpers around typed field names and local aliases, adding lbmthp-specific assert/report macros and named static data references, and fixing THPVideoDecode call-site pointer typing in lb code. The bot report recorded 3 new matches and 5 unmatched-item improvements for main/melee/lb/lbmthp.

key_files.role: Primary matching work: typed THP/movie-player field accesses, helper refactors, lbmthp assert/report macros, named static data references, rate-table helper extraction, and THPVideoDecode call cleanup.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/lb/lbmthp.c +183/-202, 10 hunks

diff_excerpt: LBMTHP_ASSERT/LBMTHP_ASSERTREPORT added; Movieplayer accesses rewritten through streamPlayer; unk_128/unk_120/unk_4C renamed to file_entrynum/curr_file_offset/frame_buffers; lbMthp_GetFrame extracted
text_corpus: bot report lists new matches and improvements in main/melee/lb/lbmthp

what_changed.text: Added lbmthp-local assert/report wrappers that preserve lbmthp-specific assert filename/message data and OSReport side effects.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: #define LBMTHP_ASSERT and LBMTHP_ASSERTREPORT
diff_excerpt: HSD_ASSERT/HSD_ASSERTREPORT uses replaced with LBMTHP_ASSERT/LBMTHP_ASSERTREPORT

what_changed.text: Replaced many inline OSReport/assert string literals in lbmthp.c with named static labels/string symbols.
evidence_refs: pr_body: 'replace literal/assert data references with named static data'
diff_excerpt: OSReport string literals changed to str_Warning_frame_offsets_not_supported, lbl_803BAE3C, lbl_803BAE68, lbl_803BAE80, etc.

what_changed.text: Refactored repeated frame-rate table conversion logic in fn_8001F2A4 into a static inline helper lbMthp_GetFrame.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: static inline u32 lbMthp_GetFrame(u32** rate_table, u32 counter) added; repeated conversion blocks removed from fn_8001F2A4

smart_moves.text: Using a local streamPlayer pointer to &Movieplayer made repeated global struct accesses easier to type and kept field names consistent across callback/setup paths.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: struct lbl_804333E0_t* streamPlayer = &Movieplayer in fn_8001E910 and lbMthp_8001F410
diff_excerpt: Movieplayer.unk_* accesses rewritten as streamPlayer->typed_or_unk fields

smart_moves.text: Extracting lbMthp_GetFrame removed duplicated rate-table conversion code while preserving the rate-table walk shape in one helper.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: three duplicated conversion blocks in fn_8001F2A4 replaced by calls to lbMthp_GetFrame

smart_moves.text: Renaming only locally supported fields such as file_entrynum, curr_file_offset, frame_buffers, and rate_table improved readability while leaving many unknown fields as unk_*.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: selected semantic field names introduced while surrounding fields remain unk_13C, unk_134, unk_130, unk_108, etc.
decomp_standards: conservative-naming

smart_moves.text: The THPVideoDecode signature/call-site cleanup changed an address-like argument from an integer cast to a pointer cast, aligning call-site type intent with pointer usage.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: (s32) lbl_804335B8.unk94 -> (void*) lbl_804335B8.unk94
diff_excerpt: frame buffer decode input cast to void*

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a global state object is accessed repeatedly, introducing a local typed pointer can help expose supported field roles and reduce noisy global-address access patterns, but unsupported fields should remain unk_*.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: streamPlayer local pointer added and selected Movieplayer fields renamed
decomp_standards: conservative-naming
decomp_standards: typed-fields-over-pointer-math

decomp_lessons.lesson: Repeated generated-looking arithmetic over a table can be a real helper candidate when the same conversion is used multiple times in one function.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: duplicated fn_8001F2A4 rate-table conversion replaced by static inline lbMthp_GetFrame
decomp_standards: natural-loops
decomp_standards: infer-authored-source-style

decomp_lessons.lesson: Signature cleanup should propagate to nearby call sites: changing THPVideoDecode input semantics to pointer form required the lb_01F8.c call site and lbmthp.c decode call to pass void* rather than integer-like values.
evidence_refs: pr_body: THPVideoDecode pointer signature/call site fix
diff_excerpt: lb_01F8.c and lbmthp.c THPVideoDecode arguments cast to void*
decomp_standards: truthful-headers-and-includes

accepted_candidate_records.title: Local state pointer can clarify repeated global struct field accesses
accepted_candidate_records.text: In lbmthp.c, repeated Movieplayer accesses were rewritten through a local streamPlayer pointer, enabling selected field names like file_entrynum, curr_file_offset, frame_buffers, and rate_table while preserving unknown unk_* fields. Treat as a local matching/readability tactic, not a universal rule.
